content_en:
And at the end of the hadith, the Prophet #% related that he said :

“You took me about tonight, so now inform me about what I ") Related by Bukhaaree (6095), Muslim (59), Ahmad (8470), At-Tirmidhee (2631), and An-Nassaaee (5021).

saw.” They answered him, ‘“Yes, as for the one you saw having his mouth ripped apart, he is a liar; he speaks a lie, and it is conveyed from him until it reaches the various regions [of the earth]. That (i.e., what you saw being done to him) will be done to him until the Day of Resurrection...)

Related Issue: The greatest lies are to lie upon Allah, to lie upon His Messenger #s, and to lie when swearing by Allah 4 in order to cheat a Muslim out of his wealth (or rights).

But what does it mean to lie upon Allah 3¢? One lies upon Allah by interpreting His Speech without knowledge. Our pious predecessors avoided interpreting Allah’s Speech without knowledge, which they made evident in practice and in many of their sayings. Abu Bakr As-Siddeeq # said, “What earth will carry me and what sky will give me shade if I say about Allah’s Book that which I do not know.” Masroog said, ‘“Be afraid of AtTafseer (explaining the meaning of the Quran), for it is narrating from Allah 4.” After relating these and other similar sayings, Ibn Taymiyyah said, ‘These and similar narrations from the Imams of our pious predecessors illustrate the care they took not to say in Tafseer that regarding which they had no knowledge. But if one speaks about something he knows - for example, a language related issue or a point from the Shariah - then there is no sin upon him.”

And how does one lie upon the Messenger of Allah #? One lies upon him 2% by fabricating a hadith and then falsely ascribing it to the Prophet #, by claiming that the Prophet #% said certain words or did a certain deed. The person who lies upon the Prophet #% is promised the Hellfire. ‘Alee Ibn Abee Taalib reported that the Prophet 2% said,

GO gil gle 215 ff 28 “Do not lie upon me, for whosoever lies upon me enters the Hellfire.”

" Related by Bukhaaree (1386) and Ahmad (19652). "I Related by Bukhaaree (106), Muslim (1), Ahmad (630), At-Tirmidhee

I mentioned that taking a false oath in order to take possession of a Muslim’s wealth is one of the worst forms of lying. ‘Abdullah Ibn Mas’ood reported that the Prophet #% said,

“Whoever swears a false oath in order to take possession of the wealth of a Muslim man...then he will meet Allah 3%, and Allah 3€ will be angry with him...’")

‘Abdullah Ibn ‘Amr 4 reported that the Prophet 2% said,

“The Kabaair (major sins) - [they are] associating partners with Allah, undutifulness to one's parents, killing a person, and Al-

Yameen Al-Ghamoos”! (to take a false oath in order to take possession of the wealth of one’s Muslim brother).""9

And it is related that Ibn Mas’ood 2 said, “We would consider Al-Yameen Al-Ghamoos a sin for which there is no atonement: it is for a man to swear upon the wealth of his brother, lying in order to take possession of it”!

(2660), and Ibn Maajah (31). The wording mentioned above is from Bukhaaree’s narration of the hadith.

"I Related by Bukhaaree (6659), Muslim (138), Ahmad (3566), At-Tirmidhee (1269), Abu Daawood (3243), and Ibn Maajah (2323), And the wording mentioned above is from Bukhaaree’s narration of the hadith.

1 Ghamoos is from the verb Ghamasa, which means to plunge or immerse.

This kind of false oath is called Ghamoos because it plunges or immerses

its perpetrator into the Hellfire.

Related by Bukhaaree (6675), Ahmad (6845), At-Tirmidhee (3621), An-

Nassaaee (4011), and Ad-Daarimee (2360). And the wording mentioned

above is from Bukhaaree’s narration of the hadith.

4] Tbn Hajr said, “Adam Ibn lyaas related this in the Musnad of Sho’bah, and Ismaa’eel Al-Qaadee related it in Al-Ahkaam from Ibn Mas‘ood .” (Fathul-Baaree 11/566)

Another Related Issue: Lying is permissible in three matters:

1) Bringing people together and resolving differences between them.

2) Lying during war.

3) When a man speaks to his wife or when a woman speaks to her husband (to appease him or her and to bring harmony to their relationship, by expressing a great deal of love even if one does not feel all of that love).

The basis for this ruling is the hadith of Umm Kalthoom Bint “‘Ugbah Ibn Abee Mu’eet #, in which she %& said, ‘I heard the Messenger of Allah # say,

.4 of eet a By ee . 4 Sat wit Oy STE god ON BS ALA! cdl Sts pty

“He is not a liar who resolves differences between people, promoting goodness or saying good words. va

In Abu Daawood’s narration, she % said, ‘I have not heard the Messenger of Allah #& license people to lie in anything except for three matters. The Messenger of Allah ¢ would say,

LNG Oy b8 Oi ge Atl Leon Ge et

Wasi) bs “T do not consider to be a liar a man who resolves differences between people: he who says something, intending nothing other than to bring harmony between them; a man who speaks about war; and a man who speaks to his wife or a woman who speaks to her husband (words with which he or she intends to increase the love that exists between them).’"7!

The people of knowledge disagree about the intended meaning of the hadith. The majority of scholars hold that it is permissible to lie in the three said matters; some scholars are of the view that

the meaning of lying is not outright lying, but playing on words, whereby the literal meaning of what one says is true, though there are two possible meanings that one intends, one that is for the listener and one is a meaning that he keeps hidden in his breast. The former view, that of the majority of scholars, is the correct view in this issue.

Other Ahadeeth attest to Abu Daawood’s narration; one example is the above-mentioned narration of Umm Kalthoom 4, in which the Prophet 2 said, “He is not a liar who resolves differences between people, promoting goodness or saying good words”; another example is the following hadith, which is related by Jaabir Ibn ‘Abdullah . In it, the Prophet #% was seeking the death of Ka’ab Ibn Al-Ashraf, who had cursed the Prophet and deceived him by breaking his promise not to help outside forces against the Muslims. Muhammad [bn Musailamah 2 volunteered to punish Ka’ab for his treachery, and after speaking in private with the Prophet #, he 4 announced, “Wa ‘Annaanaa,”” which literally means ‘he has burdened us and charged us with a task.’ The meaning Muhammad Ibn Musailamah # intended to convey to those nearby was that the Prophet #% had charged him with a burdensome, boring task; he was not lying, for the Prophet #% had indeed charged him with a task, but it was neither burdensome nor boring; instead, it was important and pleasing. This example relates to lying or playing on words during war or for a war-like purpose.

There is even a narration that attests to the last part of Abu Daawood’s narration: about lying to one’s spouse in order to appease him or her. ‘Ataa Ibn Yasaar said, “A man went to the Prophet # and said, ‘O Messenger of Allah, is there sin upon me for lying to my wife?’ He #¢ said,

“Allah does not love lying.’

He said, ‘O Messenger of Allah, [it was] to make peace with her and placate her soul.’ The Prophet #¢ said,

Chapter - 8 The Manners Of Speech

“{Then] there is no sin upon you.)

An-Nawawee said, “Concerning a man lying to his wife and her lying to him, what is meant is a demonstration or a show of love, a promise of what is not binding, or something similar. On the other hand, lying to deceive - when he or she is trying to withhold the other’s right -is Haram (forbidden) by the consensus of the Muslims. And Allah 3% knows best.”

And Al-Albaanee said, “It is not a form of permissible lying for a man to promise his wife something when he does not intend to fulfill that promise. Nor is it permissible for him to tell her that he bought something for a certain price, telling her so in order to please her, when he really paid a lesser sum. If she were to find him out, she would begin to harbor suspicious about him, and so his lie ends up not in reconciliation, but in distrust and strife.”

8) The Prohibition Of Lewdness, Obscenity, And Foul Language

Having the most complete set of manners and characteristics, the Prophet #% was furthest from base and foul language. He 2% forbade lewdness, cursing, and all falseness in speech. Ibn Mas’ood 4 reported that the Prophet # said,

tesa pod V5 ot V5 cots 3.4 och “The believer is not a Ta’aan (slanderer; one who attacks the

honor of others through disparaging words and backbiting); nor is he a curser or [a speaker] of lewd and base language.)

Al-Fuhsh, the Arabic word for lewdness, can be used to signify different meanings. It can, in a general sense, mean cursing and

‘| Al-Albaanee said in As-Saheehah, “Related by Al-Humaidee in his Musnad (# 329) (As-Silsilah, 1/817) (#498). The hadith is Mursal, but one may refer to the said reference to see why Al-Albaanee included the narration in his As-Silsilah As-Saheehah.

I Related by Ahmad (3938), At-Tirmidhee (1977), and Bukhaaree in AlAdab Al-Mufrad. The wording mentioned above is from Bukhaaree’s narration, which Al-Albaanee ruled to be authentic.

lewd language, as in the hadith of ‘Abdullah Ibn ‘Amr 4. He said, ‘The Prophet #% was neither Faahish nor Mutafaahish (i.e., he did not curse or speak lewdly), and he #% used to say, ‘Verily, from the best among you is the one who is best among you in manners./”"1]

Al-Fuhsh can also mean, transgressing the boundaries of good etiquette in conversation, a connotation of Fuhsh that is intended in the following hadith. ‘Aaisha ‘ said, “A group of Jews came and said, ‘As-Saam (i.e., death; however it is close in pronunciation to ‘As-Salaam,’ which means peace; so they were trying to trick the Prophet #%)’ upon you, O Abul-Qaasim (i.e., Muhammad #%).’ The Prophet #% answered, ‘And the same upon you.’ I then said to them, ‘Rather, upon you As-Saam (death) and Adh-Dhaam (shame, disgrace).’ The Messenger of Allah #8 said,
content_id:
Dan di akhir hadith, Nabi ﷺ menceritakan bahwa beliau berkata:

"Kalian membawaku berkeliling malam ini, maka sekarang beritahukan kepadaku tentang apa yang aku ") Diriwayatkan oleh Bukhaaree (6095), Muslim (59), Ahmad (8470), At-Tirmidhee (2631), dan An-Nassaaee (5021).

lihat." Mereka menjawab, "Ya, adapun orang yang kamu lihat mulutnya dirobek, dia adalah pendusta; dia berbicara dusta, dan itu disebarkan darinya hingga mencapai berbagai penjuru [bumi]. Itu (yakni, apa yang kamu lihat dilakukan kepadanya) akan terus dilakukan kepadanya hingga Hari Kiamat...)

Masalah Terkait: Dusta yang paling besar adalah berdusta atas nama Allah, berdusta atas nama Rasul-Nya ﷺ, dan berdusta ketika bersumpah atas nama Allah ﷻ untuk merampas harta (atau hak) seorang Muslim.

Namun apa artinya berdusta atas nama Allah ﷻ? Seseorang berdusta atas nama Allah dengan menafsirkan Firman-Nya tanpa ilmu. Para pendahulu kita yang saleh menghindari menafsirkan Firman Allah tanpa ilmu, yang mereka tunjukkan dalam praktik dan dalam banyak ucapan mereka. Abu Bakr As-Siddeeq رضي الله عنه berkata, "Bumi mana yang akan menanggungku dan langit mana yang akan menaungi jika aku berkata tentang Kitab Allah sesuatu yang tidak aku ketahui." Masrooq berkata, "Takutlah terhadap At-Tafseer (menjelaskan makna Quran), karena itu adalah meriwayatkan dari Allah ﷻ." Setelah menyebutkan ucapan-ucapan ini dan yang serupa, Ibn Taymiyyah berkata, "Narasi-narasi ini dan yang serupa dari para Imam pendahulu kita yang saleh menggambarkan kehati-hatian mereka untuk tidak mengatakan dalam Tafseer hal-hal yang tidak mereka ketahui. Namun jika seseorang berbicara tentang sesuatu yang ia ketahui - misalnya, masalah bahasa atau poin dari Syariah - maka tidak ada dosa atasnya."

Dan bagaimana seseorang berdusta atas nama Rasulullah ﷺ? Seseorang berdusta atas namanya ﷺ dengan memalsukan sebuah hadith lalu menisbatkannya secara palsu kepada Nabi ﷺ, dengan mengklaim bahwa Nabi ﷺ mengucapkan kata-kata tertentu atau melakukan perbuatan tertentu. Orang yang berdusta atas nama Nabi ﷺ dijanjikan masuk Neraka. 'Alee Ibn Abee Taalib meriwayatkan bahwa Nabi ﷺ bersabda,

GO gil gle 215 ff 28 "Janganlah berdusta atasku, karena barangsiapa berdusta atasku maka ia masuk Neraka."

" Diriwayatkan oleh Bukhaaree (1386) dan Ahmad (19652). " Diriwayatkan oleh Bukhaaree (106), Muslim (1), Ahmad (630), At-Tirmidhee

Saya menyebutkan bahwa mengambil sumpah palsu untuk menguasai harta seorang Muslim adalah salah satu bentuk dusta yang paling buruk. 'Abdullah Ibn Mas'ood meriwayatkan bahwa Nabi ﷺ bersabda,

"Barangsiapa bersumpah palsu untuk menguasai harta seorang Muslim...maka dia akan menemui Allah ﷻ, dan Allah ﷻ akan murka kepadanya...'")

'Abdullah Ibn 'Amr رضي الله عنه meriwayatkan bahwa Nabi ﷺ bersabda,

"Al-Kabaair (dosa-dosa besar) - [yaitu] menyekutukan Allah, durhaka kepada orang tua, membunuh seseorang, dan Al-

Yameen Al-Ghamoos" (bersumpah palsu untuk menguasai harta saudara Muslim seseorang).""9

Dan diriwayatkan bahwa Ibn Mas'ood رضي الله عنه berkata, "Kami menganggap Al-Yameen Al-Ghamoos sebagai dosa yang tidak ada kaffaratnya: yaitu seseorang bersumpah atas harta saudaranya, berdusta untuk menguasainya"!

(2660), dan Ibn Maajah (31). Redaksi yang disebutkan di atas berasal dari riwayat Bukhaaree tentang hadith tersebut.

"' Diriwayatkan oleh Bukhaaree (6659), Muslim (138), Ahmad (3566), At-Tirmidhee (1269), Abu Daawood (3243), dan Ibn Maajah (2323), Dan redaksi yang disebutkan di atas berasal dari riwayat Bukhaaree tentang hadith tersebut.

1 Ghamoos berasal dari kata kerja Ghamasa, yang berarti menyelam atau menenggelamkan.

Jenis sumpah palsu ini disebut Ghamoos karena menyelam atau menenggelamkan

pelakunya ke dalam Neraka.

Diriwayatkan oleh Bukhaaree (6675), Ahmad (6845), At-Tirmidhee (3621), An-

Nassaaee (4011), dan Ad-Daarimee (2360). Dan redaksi yang disebutkan

di atas berasal dari riwayat Bukhaaree tentang hadith tersebut.

4] Ibn Hajr berkata, "Adam Ibn Iyaas meriwayatkan ini dalam Musnad Sho'bah, dan Ismaa'eel Al-Qaadee meriwayatkannya dalam Al-Ahkaam dari Ibn Mas'ood رضي الله عنه." (Fathul-Baaree 11/566)

Masalah Terkait Lainnya: Dusta diperbolehkan dalam tiga hal:

1) Mendamaikan orang-orang dan menyelesaikan perselisihan di antara mereka.

2) Dusta dalam perang.

3) Ketika seorang suami berbicara kepada istrinya atau ketika seorang istri berbicara kepada suaminya (untuk menenangkan atau mendamaikannya dan membawa keharmonisan dalam hubungan mereka, dengan mengungkapkan banyak cinta meskipun seseorang tidak merasakan semua cinta itu).

Dasar hukum ini adalah hadith Umm Kalthoom Bint 'Uqbah Ibn Abee Mu'eet رضي الله عنها, di mana beliau رضي الله عنها berkata, "Aku mendengar Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda,

.4 of eet a By ee . 4 Sat wit Oy STE god ON BS ALA! cdl Sts pty

"Bukanlah pendusta orang yang mendamaikan di antara manusia, menyebarkan kebaikan atau mengucapkan kata-kata yang baik."

Dalam riwayat Abu Daawood, beliau رضي الله عنها berkata, "Aku tidak pernah mendengar Rasulullah ﷺ memberi rukhshah kepada manusia untuk berdusta dalam hal apa pun kecuali dalam tiga hal. Rasulullah ﷺ biasa bersabda,

LNG Oy b8 Oi ge Atl Leon Ge et

Wasi) bs "Aku tidak menganggap pendusta seorang laki-laki yang mendamaikan di antara manusia: yaitu yang mengatakan sesuatu, tidak bertujuan selain untuk mendamaikan di antara mereka; seorang laki-laki yang berbicara tentang perang; dan seorang laki-laki yang berbicara kepada istrinya atau seorang perempuan yang berbicara kepada suaminya (kata-kata yang dengannya ia bermaksud meningkatkan cinta yang ada di antara mereka).'"7!

Para ulama berbeda pendapat tentang maksud dari hadith tersebut. Mayoritas ulama berpendapat bahwa dusta diperbolehkan dalam tiga hal yang disebutkan; sebagian ulama berpendapat bahwa

makna dusta di sini bukanlah dusta terang-terangan, melainkan permainan kata-kata, di mana makna literal dari apa yang diucapkan seseorang adalah benar, meskipun ada dua kemungkinan makna yang dimaksudkan, satu untuk pendengar dan satu makna yang disembunyikan dalam dadanya. Pendapat pertama, yaitu pendapat mayoritas ulama, adalah pendapat yang benar dalam masalah ini.

Hadith-hadith lain menguatkan riwayat Abu Daawood; salah satu contohnya adalah riwayat Umm Kalthoom رضي الله عنها yang disebutkan di atas, di mana Nabi ﷺ bersabda, "Bukanlah pendusta orang yang mendamaikan di antara manusia, menyebarkan kebaikan atau mengucapkan kata-kata yang baik"; contoh lainnya adalah hadith berikut, yang diriwayatkan oleh Jaabir Ibn 'Abdullah رضي الله عنه. Di dalamnya, Nabi ﷺ sedang mencari kematian Ka'ab Ibn Al-Ashraf, yang telah mencaci Nabi dan menipunya dengan mengingkari janjinya untuk tidak membantu pihak luar melawan kaum Muslimin. Muhammad Ibn Musailamah رضي الله عنه menawarkan diri untuk menghukum Ka'ab atas pengkhianatannya, dan setelah berbicara secara pribadi dengan Nabi ﷺ, beliau رضي الله عنه mengumumkan, "Wa 'Annaanaa," yang secara harfiah berarti 'dia telah membebani kami dan membebankan tugas kepada kami.' Makna yang dimaksudkan Muhammad Ibn Musailamah رضي الله عنه untuk disampaikan kepada orang-orang di sekitarnya adalah bahwa Nabi ﷺ telah memberinya tugas yang memberatkan dan membosankan; dia tidak berdusta, karena Nabi ﷺ memang telah memberinya tugas, namun tugas itu tidak memberatkan atau membosankan; sebaliknya, itu penting dan menyenangkan. Contoh ini berkaitan dengan dusta atau permainan kata-kata dalam perang atau untuk tujuan yang menyerupai perang.

Bahkan ada riwayat yang menguatkan bagian terakhir dari riwayat Abu Daawood: tentang berbohong kepada pasangan untuk menenangkannya. 'Ataa Ibn Yasaar berkata, "Seorang laki-laki datang kepada Nabi ﷺ dan berkata, 'Wahai Rasulullah, apakah ada dosa atasku karena berdusta kepada istriku?' Beliau ﷺ bersabda,

"Allah tidak menyukai dusta.'

Dia berkata, 'Wahai Rasulullah, [itu] untuk berdamai dengannya dan menenangkan jiwanya.' Nabi ﷺ bersabda,

Bab - 8 Adab Berbicara

"[Kalau begitu] tidak ada dosa atasmu.")

An-Nawawee berkata, "Mengenai seorang suami yang berdusta kepada istrinya dan istrinya yang berdusta kepadanya, yang dimaksud adalah ungkapan atau pertunjukan cinta, janji tentang sesuatu yang tidak mengikat, atau hal serupa. Di sisi lain, dusta untuk menipu - ketika suami atau istri mencoba menahan hak pasangannya - adalah haram berdasarkan ijma' kaum Muslimin. Dan Allah ﷻ lebih mengetahui."

Dan Al-Albaanee berkata, "Bukan merupakan bentuk dusta yang diperbolehkan bagi seorang suami untuk berjanji kepada istrinya sesuatu yang tidak ia berniat penuhi. Tidak pula diperbolehkan baginya untuk mengatakan kepadanya bahwa dia membeli sesuatu dengan harga tertentu, mengatakannya untuk menyenangkannya, padahal dia sebenarnya membayar dengan jumlah yang lebih sedikit. Jika dia mengetahuinya, dia akan mulai menaruh kecurigaan terhadapnya, sehingga dustanya berakhir bukan dengan perdamaian, melainkan dengan ketidakpercayaan dan perselisihan."

8) Larangan Kekejian, Kemunkarah, dan Bahasa Kotor

Memiliki perangai dan akhlak yang paling sempurna, Nabi ﷺ adalah yang paling jauh dari bahasa rendah dan kotor. Beliau ﷺ melarang kekejian, kutukan, dan segala kepalsuan dalam berbicara. Ibn Mas'ood رضي الله عنه meriwayatkan bahwa Nabi ﷺ bersabda,

tesa pod V5 ot V5 cots 3.4 och "Seorang mukmin bukanlah seorang Ta'aan (pencela; orang yang menyerang kehormatan orang lain melalui kata-kata yang merendahkan dan ghibah); dan bukan pula seorang pelaknat atau [pembicara] bahasa keji dan rendah.)

Al-Fuhsh, kata Arab untuk kekejian, dapat digunakan untuk menunjukkan berbagai makna. Secara umum, kata ini dapat berarti melaknat dan

'| Al-Albaanee berkata dalam As-Saheehah, "Diriwayatkan oleh Al-Humaidee dalam Musnad-nya (# 329) (As-Silsilah, 1/817) (#498). Hadith ini Mursal, namun seseorang dapat merujuk kepada referensi tersebut untuk melihat mengapa Al-Albaanee memasukkan periwayatan ini dalam As-Silsilah As-Saheehah-nya.

I Diriwayatkan oleh Ahmad (3938), At-Tirmidhee (1977), dan Bukhaaree dalam Al-Adab Al-Mufrad. Redaksi yang disebutkan di atas berasal dari riwayat Bukhaaree, yang dinilai shahih oleh Al-Albaanee.

bahasa keji, sebagaimana dalam hadith 'Abdullah Ibn 'Amr 4. Beliau berkata, 'Nabi #% tidak Faahish dan tidak pula Mutafaahish (yakni, beliau tidak melaknat atau berbicara keji), dan beliau #% biasa bersabda, 'Sesungguhnya, termasuk yang terbaik di antara kalian adalah orang yang paling baik akhlaknya di antara kalian.'/""1]

Al-Fuhsh juga dapat berarti melampaui batas-batas etika yang baik dalam percakapan, suatu konotasi Fuhsh yang dimaksudkan dalam hadith berikut. 'Aaisha ' berkata, "Sekelompok orang Yahudi datang dan berkata, 'As-Saam (yakni, kematian; namun pelafazannya mirip dengan 'As-Salaam,' yang berarti kedamaian; sehingga mereka mencoba menipu Nabi #%)' atasmu, wahai Abul-Qaasim (yakni, Muhammad #%).' Nabi #% menjawab, 'Dan hal yang sama atasmu.' Aku kemudian berkata kepada mereka, 'Bahkan, atas kalian As-Saam (kematian) dan Adh-Dhaam (kehinaan, keburukan).' Rasulullah #8 bersabda,
content_ar: And at the end of the الحديث, النبي ﷺ #% related that قال : “You took me about tonight, لذا now inform me about what I ") Related by Bukhaaree (6095), مسلم (59), أحمد (8470), At-Tirmidhee (2631), and An-Nassaaee (5021). saw.” They answered him, ‘“Yes, أما the one you saw having his mouth ripped apart, he is a liar; he speaks a lie, and it is conveyed from him حتى it reaches the various regions [of the earth]. That (i.e., what you saw being done to him) will be done to him حتى the Day of البعث...) Related Issue: The greatest lies are to lie upon الله, to lie upon His Messenger #s, and to lie عندما swearing by الله 4 من أجل cheat a مسلم out of his الثروة (or الحقوق). But what does it mean to lie upon الله 3¢? One lies upon الله by interpreting His Speech without العلم. Our pious predecessors avoided interpreting الله’s Speech without العلم, which they made evident in practice and in many of their sayings. أبو بكر As-Siddeeq # said, “What earth will carry me and what sky will give me shade إذا I say about الله’s الكتاب that which I do not know.” Masroog said, ‘“Be afraid of AtTafseer (explaining the meaning of the القرآن الكريم), for it is narrating from الله 4.” بعد relating these and other similar sayings, ابن تيمية said, ‘These and similar narrations from the Imams of our pious predecessors illustrate the care they took not to say in Tafseer that فيما يخص which they had no العلم. But إذا one speaks about something he knows - على سبيل المثال, a language related issue or a point from the Shariah - then there is no الذنب upon him.” And how does one lie upon الرسول ﷺ of الله #? One lies upon him 2% by fabricating a الحديث and then falsely ascribing it to النبي ﷺ #, by claiming that النبي ﷺ #% said certain words or did a certain deed. The person who lies upon النبي ﷺ #% is promised the النار. ‘Alee Ibn Abee Taalib reported that النبي ﷺ 2% said, GO gil gle 215 ff 28 “Do not lie upon me, for whosoever lies upon me enters the النار.” " Related by Bukhaaree (1386) and أحمد (19652). "I Related by Bukhaaree (106), مسلم (1), أحمد (630), At-Tirmidhee I mentioned that taking a false oath من أجل take possession of a مسلم’s الثروة is one of the worst forms of lying. ‘Abdullah Ibn Mas’ood reported that النبي ﷺ #% said, “Whoever swears a false oath من أجل take possession of the الثروة of a مسلم man...then he will meet الله 3%, and الله 3€ will be angry with him...’") ‘Abdullah Ibn ‘Amr 4 reported that النبي ﷺ 2% said, “The Kabaair (major الذنوب) - [they are] associating partners with الله, undutifulness to one's الوالدان, killing a person, and Al- Yameen Al-Ghamoos”! (to take a false oath من أجل take possession of the الثروة of one’s مسلم الأخ).""9 And it is related that Ibn Mas’ood 2 said, “We would consider Al-Yameen Al-Ghamoos a الذنب for which there is no atonement: it is for a man to swear upon the الثروة of his الأخ, lying من أجل take possession of it”! (2660), and Ibn Maajah (31). The wording mentioned above is from Bukhaaree’s narration of the الحديث. "I Related by Bukhaaree (6659), مسلم (138), أحمد (3566), At-Tirmidhee (1269), Abu Daawood (3243), and Ibn Maajah (2323), And the wording mentioned above is from Bukhaaree’s narration of the الحديث. 1 Ghamoos is from the verb Ghamasa, أي to plunge or immerse. This kind of false oath is called Ghamoos لأن it plunges or immerses its perpetrator into the النار. Related by Bukhaaree (6675), أحمد (6845), At-Tirmidhee (3621), An- Nassaaee (4011), and Ad-Daarimee (2360). And the wording mentioned above is from Bukhaaree’s narration of the الحديث. 4] Tbn Hajr said, “آدم Ibn lyaas related this in the Musnad of Sho’bah, and Ismaa’eel Al-Qaadee related it in Al-Ahkaam from Ibn Mas‘ood .” (Fathul-Baaree 11/566) Another Related Issue: Lying is permissible in three matters: 1) Bringing people together and resolving differences between them. 2) Lying خلال war. 3) عندما a man speaks to his الزوجة or عندما a woman speaks to her الزوج (to appease him or her and to bring harmony to their relationship, by expressing a great deal of المحبة even إذا one does not feel all of that المحبة). The basis for this ruling is the الحديث of Umm Kalthoom Bint “‘Ugbah Ibn Abee Mu’eet #, in which she %& said, ‘I heard الرسول ﷺ of الله # say, .4 of eet a By ee . 4 Sat wit Oy STE god ON BS ALA! cdl Sts pty “He is not a liar who resolves differences between people, promoting goodness or saying الخير words. va In Abu Daawood’s narration, she % said, ‘I have not heard الرسول ﷺ of الله #& license people to lie in anything except for three matters. الرسول ﷺ of الله ¢ would say, LNG Oy b8 Oi ge Atl Leon Ge et Wasi) bs “T do not consider to be a liar a man who resolves differences between people: he who says something, intending nothing other than to bring harmony between them; a man who speaks about war; and a man who speaks to his الزوجة or a woman who speaks to her الزوج (words with which he or she intends to increase the المحبة that exists between them).’"7! The people of العلم disagree about the intended meaning of the الحديث. The majority of العلماء hold that يجوز to lie in the three said matters; some العلماء are of the view that the meaning of lying is not outright lying, but playing on words, whereby the literal meaning of what one says is true, though there are two possible meanings that one intends, one that is for the listener and one is a meaning that he keeps hidden in his breast. The former view, that of the majority of العلماء, is القول الراجح in this issue. Other Ahadeeth attest to Abu Daawood’s narration; one example is the above-mentioned narration of Umm Kalthoom 4, in which النبي ﷺ 2 said, “He is not a liar who resolves differences between people, promoting goodness or saying الخير words”; another example is the following الحديث, which is related by Jaabir Ibn ‘Abdullah . In it, النبي ﷺ #% was seeking the الموت of Ka’ab Ibn Al-Ashraf, who had cursed النبي ﷺ and deceived him by breaking his promise not to help outside forces against the المسلمون. محمد ﷺ [bn Musailamah 2 volunteered to punish Ka’ab for his treachery, and بعد speaking in private with النبي ﷺ #, he 4 announced, “Wa ‘Annaanaa,”” which literally means ‘he has burdened us and charged us with a task.’ The meaning محمد ﷺ Ibn Musailamah # intended to convey to those nearby was that النبي ﷺ #% had charged him with a burdensome, boring task; he was not lying, for النبي ﷺ #% had indeed charged him with a task, but it was neither burdensome nor boring; instead, it was المهم and pleasing. This example relates to lying or playing on words خلال war or for a war-like purpose. There is even a narration that attests to the last part of Abu Daawood’s narration: about lying to one’s spouse من أجل appease him or her. ‘Ataa Ibn Yasaar said, “A man went to النبي ﷺ # and said, ‘O Messenger of الله, is there الذنب upon me for lying to my الزوجة?’ He #¢ said, “الله does not المحبة lying.’ قال, ‘O Messenger of الله, [it was] to make peace with her and placate her النفس.’ النبي ﷺ #¢ said, الباب - 8 The الآداب Of Speech “{Then] there is no الذنب upon you.) An-Nawawee said, “فيما يتعلق بـ a man lying to his الزوجة and her lying to him, what is meant is a demonstration or a show of المحبة, a promise of what is not binding, or something similar. من ناحية أخرى, lying to deceive - عندما he or she is trying to withhold the other’s الحق -is الحرام (forbidden) by the consensus of the المسلمون. And الله 3% knows best.” And Al-Albaanee said, “It is not a form of permissible lying for a man to promise his الزوجة something عندما he does not intend to fulfill that promise. Nor is it permissible for him to tell her that he bought something for a certain price, telling her لذا من أجل please her, عندما he really paid a lesser sum. إذا she were to find him out, she would begin to harbor suspicious about him, and لذا his lie ends up not in reconciliation, but in distrust and strife.” 8) The Prohibition Of Lewdness, Obscenity, And Foul Language Having the most complete set of الآداب and characteristics, النبي ﷺ #% was furthest from base and foul language. He 2% forbade lewdness, cursing, and all falseness in speech. Ibn Mas’ood 4 reported that النبي ﷺ # said, tesa pod V5 ot V5 cots 3.4 och “The المؤمن is not a Ta’aan (slanderer; one who attacks the الشرف of others through disparaging words and backbiting); nor is he a curser or [a speaker] of lewd and base language.) Al-Fuhsh, the Arabic word for lewdness, can be used to signify different meanings. It can, in a general sense, mean cursing and ‘| Al-Albaanee said in As-Saheehah, “Related by Al-Humaidee in his Musnad (# 329) (As-Silsilah, 1/817) (#498). The الحديث is Mursal, but one may refer to tقال reference to see why Al-Albaanee included the narration in his As-Silsilah As-Saheehah. I Related by أحمد (3938), At-Tirmidhee (1977), and Bukhaaree in AlAdab Al-Mufrad. The wording mentioned above is from Bukhaaree’s narration, which Al-Albaanee ruled to be authentic. lewd language, as in the الحديث of ‘Abdullah Ibn ‘Amr 4. قال, ‘النبي ﷺ #% was neither Faahish nor Mutafaahish (i.e., he did not curse or speak lewdly), and he #% used to say, ‘Verily, from the best among you is the one who is best among you in الآداب./”"1] Al-Fuhsh can أيضاً mean, transgressing the boundaries of الخير الآداب in conversation, a connotation of Fuhsh that is intended in the following الحديث. ‘Aaisha ‘ said, “A group of Jews came and said, ‘As-Saam (i.e., الموت; غير أن it is close in pronunciation to ‘As-Salaam,’ أي peace; لذا they were trying to trick النبي ﷺ #%)’ upon you, O Abul-Qaasim (i.e., محمد ﷺ #%).’ النبي ﷺ #% answered, ‘And the same upon you.’ I then said to them, ‘Rather, upon you As-Saam (الموت) and Adh-Dhaam (shame, disgrace).’ الرسول ﷺ of الله #8 said,
